mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month change in the Industrial Production Index indicates expanding manufacturing output, which directly boosts corporate earnings and signals economic strength, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward return distribution following positive INDPRO changes is not significantly above zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) over the sample period, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When the three-month average unemployment rate exceeds 5.5%, it signals a deteriorating labor market that depresses consumer spending and corporate profits, causing negative equity returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances (UNRATE > 5.5% 3mo avg), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously accommodative monetary policy (federal funds rate below 0.5%) and robust retail sales (above $600 billion) create a uniquely favorable environment for equities, as low discount rates amplify the value of strong consumption-driven earnings, producing positive forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day forward return under the joint trigger condition is not significantly greater than the unconditional mean (p < 0.05 in a one-sided test), reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: In a low unemployment regime (UNRATE below 5%), a large month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls (exceeding 0.5%) signals an over-heating labor market that triggers future monetary tightening and wage inflation, depressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: If during low unemployment (UNRATE < 5%), 20-day forward returns following PAYEMS growth > 0.5% are positive in more than 40% of occurrences, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP (GDPC1) serves as a broad measure of economic expansion; when quarterly growth is positive and above trend, it signals sustainable demand that supports corporate earnings growth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the Rank IC of GDPC1 quarterly growth on 60-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1,500 thousand units indicate a vibrant housing sector that drives employment and wealth effects, directly boosting consumer confidence and spending, leading to positive short-term equity returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward return following housing starts above 1500 is negative in more than 60% of the instances, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI level is above 280 and the federal funds rate is below 0.5%, real interest rates are deeply negative, encouraging borrowing from households and firms, which stimulates economic activity and inflates asset prices, leading to positive equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the average 60-day return under the joint condition is not significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.10), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During a high inflation environment (CPI level above 280), a significant increase in industrial production (more than 0.5% month-over-month) signals that the economy is overheating, which raises expectations of aggressive monetary tightening and crushes equity valuations, producing negative forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If in high CPI regimes (CPIAUCSL > 280), 20-day forward returns after strong INDPRO growth are positive in > 40% of cases, the hypothesis is falsified.
